title: Jalen Green Scores 36 As Suns Eliminate Warriors, Claim No. 8 Seed
summary: Jalen Green delivered 36 points on 14-of-20 shooting as the Phoenix Suns eliminated the Golden State Warriors 111-96 Friday, claiming the Western Conference's No. 8 seed and a first-round playoff matchup against defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder beginning Sunday. Green, who averaged just 13.3 points on 37% shooting against Golden State in last year's playoffs with the Houston Rockets, shot 8-of-14 from 3-point range in a pointed performance against the same opponent. "I remember what they did last year," Green said. "There's a lesson in every situation. It's never failure. You always get back up. So I for sure brought some of that energy from last year into tonight's game, and we capitalized." Devin Booker added 20 points and Jordan Goodwin had 19 points, nine rebounds and six steals. Stephen Curry, held to 17 points on 4-of-16 shooting, could not generate clean looks against a stifling Phoenix defense. Brandin Podziemski led Golden State with 23 points. Booker and Draymond Green were both assessed technical fouls and ejected late in the fourth quarter following a prolonged verbal exchange. Phoenix led by five at halftime before building a 69-53 advantage midway through the third quarter. Golden State briefly threatened when Curry's 3-pointer cut the margin to 85-78 with 9:30 remaining, but the Suns responded with seven consecutive points to put the game away. The result carried added weight given Phoenix's play-in history. The Suns blew an 11-point fourth-quarter lead in a Tuesday loss to Portland before bouncing back to avoid becoming the first team to lose both home play-in games since the format was established in 2021. "Sometimes the road's not always the one that you choose," Suns coach Jordan Ott said. "Sometimes it's the least traveled. So we found our way in. Now it's on to the next thing." Kristaps Porzingis played just 15 minutes for Golden State, managing 11 points while working through right ankle soreness sustained Wednesday against the Clippers.

New Trail Blazers owner Tom Dundon apparently doesn't want to spend more than $1.5 million per season on a head coach. That's far less than what most head coaches make, and is in line with what teams pay top assistant coaches. That figure is also far less than what top college coaches makes. That could impact Portland's ability to hire a quality leader on the sidelines. Splitter is expected to be a candidate to keep the job, if a new deal can be worked out. title: Steve Kerr Unsure If He'll Return As Warriors Head Coach Next Season
summary: Steve Kerr acknowledged the possibility of not returning as head coach of the Golden State Warriors on Friday following their loss at the Phoenix Suns in the play-in. Kerr is without a contract for the 2026-27 season, which was a purposeful decision as he evaluates his future. "I don't know what's going to happen," Kerr said. "I still love coaching, but I get it. These jobs all have an expiration date. There is a run that happens, and when the run ends, sometimes it's time for new blood and new ideas." Kerr will take a few weeks to contemplate whether he wants to keep coaching before meeting with Mike Dunleavy and Joe Lacob. "If [my time is done], then I will be nothing but grateful for the most amazing opportunity any person could have to coach this franchise in front of our fans and to coach Steph Curry, [Draymond Green], the whole group," Kerr said. "It may still go on. It may not. I don't know at this point. But we all need to step away a little bit and then reconvene." Kerr has won four NBA championships in his 12 seasons as head coach of the Warriors. Erik Spoelstra is the only coach with a longer tenure with his current team. Since winning the 2022 NBA championship, the Warriors have advanced to the second round of the playoffs twice and also missed the playoffs altogether in 2024 and 2026 .

title: Stephen Curry Interested In Signing Extension With Warriors
summary: Stephen Curry has one season remaining on his contract at $62.6 million for 2026-27 with the Golden State Warriors and he expressed interest in signing an extension. Curry, who most recently signed the one-year extension in 2024, has yet to hold talks with the Warriors. Curry was also asked if he can envision himself playing more than just the one season he has left on his contract. "Multiple, for sure," said Curry. While Curry has continued to play at a high level, he dealt with knee issues this seasons and the supporting cast around him is clearly below the true elite title contenders. But the Warriors were aggressive at the trade deadline as they held negotiations on deals for Giannis Antetokounmpo, Kawhi Leonard and Jaren Jackson Jr. The Warriors are expected to pursue a significant upgrade again in the offseason and have also been linked with LeBron James. The Warriors are also navigating the future of Steve Kerr, who is not under contract for next season. "I want Coach to be happy," Curry said. "I want him to be excited for the job. I want him to believe he's the right guy for the job. I want him to have an opportunity to enjoy what he does, whatever that means for him. Everybody's plan is their own. He knows how I feel about him."

title: Magic Secure No. 8 Seed, Will Face Pistons In First Round

"When you play with a sense of desperation and urgency, when you know you're either going home or extending your season, that's what it looks like," Magic coach Jamahl Mosley said. "There (are) no second chances." LaMelo Ball lead the Hornets with 23 points. The Hornets have now missed the playoffs for ten straight seasons. The Magic are set to face the Pistons on Sunday. They went 2-2 against Detroit in the regular season.

"Despite already being an All-WNBA caliber player, Aliyah has just scratched the surface in terms of the player she can become." Boston, who was the No. 1 pick in 2023 out of South Carolina, averaged 14.5 points, 8.5 rebounds and 3.0 assists in the WNBA. Boston was tied for sixth in MVP voting last season.

title: Ty Lue To Remain Head Coach Of Clippers 'For A Long, Long Time'
summary: Los Angeles Clippers president of basketball operations Lawrence Frank stated Friday that the franchise never considered replacing Tyronn Lue as head coach, even during their 6-21 start to the 2025-26 season. "Even at our lowest point, when we were 6-21, there never ever was any consideration of Ty not being the coach," Frank said at his end-of-season news conference. "Ty's going to be the coach here for a long, long time." Lue guided the Clippers to a 42-40 final record before a fourth-quarter collapse against the Golden State Warriors ended their season in Wednesday's play-in tournament. Lue remains under contract through 2029 and is believed to be tied with Erik Spoelstra as the NBA's second highest paid head coach behind Steve Kerr. Frank credited Lue's steadiness through a year that included significant midseason roster upheaval. "I thought Ty and the staff did an unbelievable job of, every day, showing up with a great spirit, and just kept at it," Frank said. "Ty's an incredible coach, an incredible partner. And one of the characteristics we look for, not just in staff, but also in players, are people who are driven to improve." Lue led the Cleveland Cavaliers to the 2016 NBA championship, overcoming a 3-1 Finals deficit against the 73-win Warriors, before joining the Clippers as an assistant in 2019 and being elevated to head coach in 2020 to replace Doc Rivers. He guided Los Angeles to the franchise's only conference finals appearance in 2021. The Clippers have not won a playoff series in five consecutive seasons .

title: Oscar Schmidt Passes Away At Age 68
summary: Basketball Hall of Famer Oscar Schmidt has passed away at the age of 68 due to a long battle with a brain tumor. Schmidt played in a record-tying five Olympics and four World Cups for Brazil and became the all-time leading scorer in both tournaments. "The biggest player of Brazilian basketball history bids farewell as an absolute symbol of sport, the holder of a trajectory that redefined the boundaries of what was possible in a court," the Brazil Basketball Confederation said in a statement. "His death closes an era. But his greatness remains." Schmidt retired in 2003 at 45. He passed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar to be the unofficial career highest scorer and his known tally of 49,737 points for club and country was eclipsed by LeBron James in 2024. Schmidt was inducted into the FIBA Hall of Fame in 2010 and the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame in 2013

title: Clippers Signal They Want To Keep Kawhi Leonard
summary: Los Angeles Clippers president of basketball operations Lawrence Frank publicly committed to building around Kawhi Leonard on Friday, days after the 34-year-old forward deflected questions about his own future following the team's play-in elimination. "Our plan is to win with Kawhi," Frank said at his end-of-season news conference. "At the appropriate time, we'll sit down with Kawhi, and very similar to 2024, lay out our plan. And if our goals are aligned, then we'd like to win with Kawhi." Leonard, who turns 35 in June, posted career-best scoring numbers this season at 27.9 points per game across 65 regular-season appearances, his second season with at least 60 games played since 2018-19. Despite the individual production, the Clippers missed the playoffs for the first time in a healthy Leonard season. Following Wednesday's play-in loss to the Golden State Warriors, Leonard offered little insight into his intentions. "Let me cry about this loss a little bit more," he said. "We'll have our discussions when that time comes." The Clippers executed significant roster moves at the trade deadline, sending James Harden to the Cleveland Cavaliers for Darius Garland and trading Ivica Zubac to the Indiana Pacers for a package that included Bennedict Mathurin, which were moves that raised questions about the franchise's direction. The Warriors also contacted the Clippers on a potential Leonard trade ahead of the deadline. Frank characterized the organization as competitive rather than rebuilding, pointing to 15 consecutive winning seasons, emerging young talent, draft capital and projected cap flexibility as evidence the Clippers remain positioned to contend. "As we build that bridge from competitive to contender, we've put ourselves in a very good position with emerging young players, draft capital and cap space going forward," Frank said. The Athletic reported this week that Garland and head coach Ty Lue are the only ones certain to return next season . An ongoing NBA investigation into Leonard's endorsement deal with now-bankrupt sponsor Aspiration also hung over the season. Frank maintained Friday that the probe had no bearing on operations and expressed full confidence in the organization's position. "If you know Steve [Ballmer] and know Steve's integrity, you know there's nothing to it," Frank said. "We believe and we're very confident we're on the right side of this."

title: NBA Internally Concedes Case For Shorter Season But Cites 20 Percent Revenue Loss Risk
summary: Steve Kerr and many media members have called for the NBA to shorten the regular season from its traditional 82-game schedule in hopes of reducing injuries as the modern game has become more physically demanding. This would theoretically allow the regular season to become more valuable due to scarcity and the presence of its best players in a higher percentage of games. "Privately, NBA officials won't argue against the merits of a shorter season," writes Chris Mannix at Sports Illustrated. "They will note-quickly-how much money would have to be forfeited to allow for it. Fewer games played means less revenue-roughly 20% by some estimates." In order for the season to be shortened the NBA and NBPA would have to collectively bargain it and would likely come with a loss of revenue over the short-term. The NFL is the world's most valuable sports league and currently plays a 17-game regular season. Over time, the NFL has expanded from 14 to 16 to 17 and there are now plans to move to 18 games in the near future. The NBA has attempted to be more strategic in the creation of its schedule to reduce back-to-backs and also multiple games over a short duration of nights. The NBA is in the first year of a 11-year, $76 billion media rights deal with ESPN, NBC and Amazon that runs through 2036.

title: LeBron James Has Made No Decision On Future, Retirement Remains Genuine Option
summary: LeBron James has not decided whether to continue his NBA career for the 2026-27 season, and retirement remains a genuine possibility, team and league sources granted anonymity to speak openly told The Athletic. James is a free agent this coming offseason. More revealing, the widely held belief that James would want a farewell tour, long cited as evidence that this season would not be his last, is simply false, sources said. Several sources indicated they heard that directly from James himself. The farewell tour narrative reflects a broader pattern of assumptions about the 41-year-old that have repeatedly proven wrong. People similarly believed James would be unable to adapt his game around the Los Angeles Lakers' younger stars, or that he would resist a reduced role as the organization built around Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves. "When I know, you guys will know," James said during All-Star Weekend in Los Angeles in February. "I don't know. I have no idea. I just want to live. That's all." Sources describe James as a deliberate, analytical decision-maker who does not allow emotions to dictate his judgment. His future, those sources say, will be determined on his own timeline and terms, not shaped by outside pressure or popular assumption. Family considerations will weigh heavily in whatever conclusion he reaches. James is teammates with son Bronny, whose contract runs through next season. His wife Savannah and 11-year-old daughter Zhuri have been rooted in Los Angeles since 2018. His youngest son Bryce redshirted this past season at Arizona. For now, James enters the playoffs once again as the focal point of the Lakers as they play without Doncic and Reaves due to injury. "The moment is all we have," he said Thursday, his voice hoarse. "At the end of the day, that's all that matters." If James decides he wants to continue his playing career, he is expected to choose between the Lakers, Golden State Warriors, Cleveland Cavaliers and Los Angeles Clippers .

title: LeBron James Could Consider Lakers, Warriors, Cavaliers, Clippers In Free Agency
summary: LeBron James has acknowledged that he's nearing the end of his career as a basketball player. What that end will come is still unknown. Sources close to James said that he's still undecided about playing beyond this season or not. One thing for certain is that James will be a free agent this offseason. He could stay with the Los Angeles Lakers, although James' current team is prioritizing having up to $50 million in cap space, as well as three available first-round picks to offer in trades . If James chooses to leave the Lakers, he's expected to consider the Golden State Warriors, Cleveland Cavaliers, and possibly the LA Clippers. Each of those teams could face financial challenges with signing James, however. The Warriors are dancing around the tax aprons, but could free up enough wiggle room to offer James the Non-Taxpayer MLE of about $15 million. The Cavs situation is made difficult, as they are likely to be over the first apron, and potentially still near the second apron. That could leave them in position to offer James only the veteran minimum. The Clippers are viewed as the least-likely option, but it would allow the NBA's all-time leading scorer to stay in Los Angeles. The Clippers salary flexibility is highly dependent on what the team chooses to do with re-signing a handful of free agents this summer.

title: NBA In Active Talks With Kalshi, Polymarket On Prediction Market Deal
summary: The NBA is in active discussions with prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket about a potential partnership deal, with talks gaining significant momentum in recent months, multiple sources familiar with the matter told Front Office Sports. Informal discussions have been underway for more than a year, but accelerated after Michael Selig, confirmed as chairman of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission in December, began engaging directly with leagues and signaling regulatory intent. "It was his engagement that accelerated talks," one source said. "He signaled he has every intention of putting real regulation in place with proper protections." The NBA is focused on maintaining control over which markets are permitted and securing mechanisms to monitor suspicious trading activity, sources said. The CFTC has flagged markets tied to player injuries and unsportsmanlike conduct as particularly vulnerable to manipulation. No firm timeline exists for a deal, though multiple sources said an agreement could be reached before next season. Unlike MLB, which signed exclusively with Polymarket, the NBA may partner with both platforms simultaneously, mirroring the NHL's approach. The MLB-Polymarket deal, worth up to $300 million over four years according to sources, is viewed as a potential benchmark for future agreements. "Everyone sees these dollar signs," one prediction-market industry executive said. In a sign of advancing discussions, NBA EVP Scott Kaufman-Ross presented an update on the talks to team presidents this week. Kalshi and Polymarket founders also appeared on the most-attended panel at the NBA's technology summit during All-Star Weekend in February. Adam Silver said during that weekend the league was viewing prediction markets similarly to traditional sports betting partners. The NBA currently holds official sports betting partnerships with DraftKings and FanDuel. Representatives for the NBA, CFTC, Kalshi and Polymarket declined to comment.

title: Wizards Retain Brian Keefe As Head Coach For 2026-27 Season
summary: Washington Wizards general manager Will Dawkins confirmed Thursday that head coach Brian Keefe will return for the 2026-27 season, transitioning the franchise from a deliberate rebuild toward a competitive push led by Trae Young and Anthony Davis. Keefe compiled a 43-160 record across two-plus rebuilding seasons, including a 17-65 mark this year. The Wizards have ranked last in Net Rating each of the past two seasons . After losing 26 of their final 27 games, the Wizards secured the NBA's worst record, guaranteeing no worse than the fifth pick in May's draft lottery and preserving a top-eight protected first-round pick owed to the New York Knicks. The front office credited Keefe with accelerating the development of Alex Sarr, Kyshawn George, Tre Johnson, Will Riley and Jamir Watkins during a period designed to prioritize youth over victories. Monumental Basketball president Michael Winger assessed Keefe's performance plainly in January. "I think Brian has done a remarkable job," Winger said. "He's developed young athletes. He's built a culture of competitiveness, accountability, togetherness and joy. I'm very, very pleased with the job that Brian has done." Keefe, Dawkins and Winger share deep organizational roots as all three spent formative stretches with the Oklahoma City Thunder, creating a philosophical alignment that has strengthened the coach's standing with team officials. Players have responded to Keefe's personal investment as much as his basketball acumen. Bub Carrington recalled that during the 2024 Summer League in Las Vegas, when his father was hospitalized with a blood clot, Keefe spent several days at the hospital with the family. When doctors prevented Carrington's father from flying home to Baltimore due to blood-thinning medication, Keefe volunteered to make the drive back with them. "I think that's the type of guy B.K. is," Carrington said. Keefe was elevated from lead assistant to interim head coach midway through the 2023-24 season following Wes Unseld Jr.'s dismissal, and was hired permanently in May 2024 after a formal search that included outside candidates.
